author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "Lastly, what is the justification of persecution?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: I ask you, you wretched men, if it so be that you think that your sin rests on any authority of law."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: He who sins, sins not on the authority of the law, but against the authority of the law.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But since you ask what is the justification of persecution, I ask you in turn whose voice it is that says in the psalm, "Whoso privily slandereth his neighbor, him will I cut off."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Seek therefore the reason or the measure of the persecution, and do not display your gross ignorance by finding fault in general terms with those who persecute the unrighteous.
